title: securetrace – Development Guide
text: R package observability tracing LLM agent workflows. Structured traces spans, token/cost accounting, latency monitoring, JSONL export.

title: securetrace – Development Guide
code: Trace (R6) -- root container for a full agent run   Span (R6) -- single operation (LLM call, tool use, guardrail check)     Event (S3) -- discrete point-in-time within a span     Metrics -- token counts, latency, custom values   Cost -- model pricing tables + calculation   Exporter (S3) -- JSONL, console, multi-exporter   Context -- trace/span stack via module-level environment   Integration -- auto-instrumentation for ellmer/securer/secureguard

title: Cloud-Native Observability
text: Inspect OTLP payload locally otlp_format_trace(). Point exporter_otlp() OTLP-HTTP endpoint. Pass headers authenticated collectors. Buffer traces batch_size; call flush_otlp() exit drain.
code: library(securetrace)  tr <- Trace$new("format-demo") tr$start() s <- Span$new("llm-call", type = "llm") s$start() s$set_model("claude-sonnet-4-5") s$set_tokens(input = 1500L, output = 300L) s$end() tr$add_span(s) tr$end()  otlp <- otlp_format_trace(tr$to_list(), service_name = "my-agent") str(otlp, max.level = 3) #> List of 1 #>  $ resourceSpans:List of 1 #>   ..$ :List of 2 #>   .. ..$ resource  :List of 1 #>   .. ..$ scopeSpans:List of 1 exp <- exporter_otlp(endpoint = "http://localhost:4318") with_trace("data-pipeline", exporter = exp, {   with_span("fetch", type = "tool", { record_latency(0.25) })   with_span("analyze", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(2000, 500, model = "claude-sonnet-4-5")   }) }) exp <- exporter_otlp(   endpoint = "https://tempo.example.com:4318",   headers = list(Authorization = "Bearer <token>"),   service_name = "my-r-agent" ) exp <- exporter_otlp("http://localhost:4318", batch_size = 10, max_retries = 3) for (i in seq_len(5)) {   with_trace(paste0("run-", i), exporter = exp, {     with_span("work", type = "tool", { i })   }) } flush_otlp(exp)

title: Cloud-Native Observability
text: Generate parse W3C traceparent headers. Inject traceparent outgoing requests inside active trace. Extract trace context incoming headers (case-insensitive).
code: tp <- traceparent(   trace_id = "4bf92f3577b34da6a3ce929d0e0e4736",   span_id = "00f067aa0ba902b7" ) tp #> [1] "00-4bf92f3577b34da6a3ce929d0e0e4736-00f067aa0ba902b7-01" traceparent("4bf92f3577b34da6a3ce929d0e0e4736", "00f067aa0ba902b7", sampled = FALSE) #> [1] "00-4bf92f3577b34da6a3ce929d0e0e4736-00f067aa0ba902b7-00" ctx <- parse_traceparent("00-4bf92f3577b34da6a3ce929d0e0e4736-00f067aa0ba902b7-01") ctx$trace_id #> [1] "4bf92f3577b34da6a3ce929d0e0e4736" ctx$span_id #> [1] "00f067aa0ba902b7" ctx$sampled #> [1] TRUE parse_traceparent("invalid-header") #> Warning: Invalid traceparent header format: "invalid-header" #> NULL with_trace("http-client", {   with_span("api-call", type = "tool", {     headers <- inject_headers(list("Content-Type" = "application/json"))     headers$traceparent   }) }) #> [1] "00-ec379768f898282ec0ee6483eaae57b6-2b7b2f7b71619fb0-01" incoming <- list(   "Content-Type" = "application/json",   traceparent = "00-4bf92f3577b34da6a3ce929d0e0e4736-00f067aa0ba902b7-01" ) ctx <- extract_trace_context(incoming) ctx$trace_id #> [1] "4bf92f3577b34da6a3ce929d0e0e4736" ctx$span_id #> [1] "00f067aa0ba902b7"

title: Cloud-Native Observability
text: Server: extract parent context create child spans. Client: inject context sides share one trace.
code: library(securetrace) library(plumber)  #* @post /analyze function(req, res) {   ctx <- extract_trace_context(list(traceparent = req$HTTP_TRACEPARENT))   tr <- Trace$new("plumber-analyze")   tr$start()   s <- Span$new("analyze", type = "llm", parent_id = ctx$span_id)   s$start()   s$set_model("claude-sonnet-4-5")   s$set_tokens(input = 2000L, output = 500L)   result <- list(status = "ok", answer = 42)   s$end()   tr$add_span(s)   tr$end()   export_trace(exporter_otlp("http://localhost:4318"), tr)   res$setHeader("traceparent", traceparent(tr$trace_id, s$span_id))   result } with_trace("client-workflow", {   with_span("call-plumber", type = "tool", {     resp <- httr2::request("http://localhost:8000/analyze") |>       httr2::req_headers(!!!inject_headers()) |>       httr2::req_method("POST") |>       httr2::req_perform()     httr2::resp_body_json(resp)   }) })

title: Ecosystem Integration: how sibling packages emit spans
text: securetrace observability backbone secure-r-dev ecosystem. sibling package (securer, securetools, secureguard, securecontext, orchestr, securebench) can emit structured spans trace context open [with_trace()] — plumbing required side. vignette documents contract siblings use know expect instrument code way.

title: Ecosystem Integration: how sibling packages emit spans
text: securetrace ships single private helper every sibling calls: sibling guards every instrumented operation helper. trace active, operation wrapped securetrace::with_span(name, type, { ... }) one .span_event() attributes attached. trace active, operation runs unchanged — zero overhead. means: sibling packages Suggest securetrace (soft dependency). Nothing emits spans unless opened trace [with_trace()] (one convenience helpers like [trace_graph()] [trace_agent()]). Cross-package traces work naturally: open trace call code crosses package boundaries, package’s spans nest .
code: .trace_active()  # TRUE iff there is a current_trace() on the stack

title: Ecosystem Integration: how sibling packages emit spans
text: table canonical list spans emitted sibling current release. Span names follow package.operation type follows [Span] taxonomy (llm, tool, guardrail, custom). span expect missing, check () sibling package installed, (b) actually opened trace around call. simplest smoke-test :
code: result <- securetrace::with_trace("smoke", {   securetools::tool_calculator()@fn("2 + 2") }) length(result$spans)  # should be > 0 if securetools is installed

title: Ecosystem Integration: how sibling packages emit spans
text: build package top ecosystem want emit spans traces, follow pattern siblings use: keeps securetrace Suggests-dependency avoids overhead consumers tracing.
code: #' @keywords internal .trace_active <- function() {   requireNamespace("securetrace", quietly = TRUE) &&     !is.null(securetrace::current_trace()) }  my_operation <- function(args) {   .do <- function() {     # ... real work ...   }   if (.trace_active()) {     securetrace::with_span("mypkg.operation", type = "custom", {       result <- .do()       securetrace:::.span_event("mypkg.operation.complete", list(         size = length(result)       ))       result     })   } else {     .do()   } }

title: Exporters
text: Write trace one JSON line append-file. Read back jsonlite:
code: library(securetrace) trace_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".jsonl") exp <- exporter_jsonl(trace_file) with_trace("exported-run", exporter = exp, {   with_span("llm-call", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(1500, 300, model = "claude-sonnet-4-5")     "response"   })   with_span("tool-call", type = "tool", { 42 }) }) #> [1] 42 lines <- readLines(trace_file) length(lines) #> [1] 1 trace_data <- jsonlite::fromJSON(lines[[1]]) trace_data$name #> [1] "exported-run" length(trace_data$spans$span_id) #> [1] 2 unlink(trace_file)

title: Exporters
text: Print trace summaries interactive development. Set verbose = FALSE headers .
code: debug_exp <- exporter_console(verbose = TRUE) with_trace("debug-run", exporter = debug_exp, {   with_span("planning", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(2000, 500, model = "claude-sonnet-4-5")     "plan"   })   with_span("execution", type = "tool", { 100 }) }) #> --- Trace: debug-run --- #> Status: completed #> Duration: 0.00s #> Spans: 2 #> -- Spans -- #>   * planning [llm] (ok) - 0.000s #>   * execution [tool] (ok) - 0.000s #> [1] 100

title: Exporters
text: Fan N destinations exporter_multi().
code: trace_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".jsonl") combined <- exporter_multi(   exporter_jsonl(trace_file),   exporter_console(verbose = TRUE) ) with_trace("multi-export-run", exporter = combined, {   with_span("llm-call", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(3000, 600, model = "claude-opus-4-6")     "result"   }) }) #> --- Trace: multi-export-run --- #> Status: completed #> Duration: 0.00s #> Spans: 1 #> -- Spans -- #>   * llm-call [llm] (ok) - 0.000s #> [1] "result" readLines(trace_file) |> length() #> [1] 1 unlink(trace_file)

title: Exporters
text: Set session-wide exporter instead passing one per with_trace() call.
code: trace_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".jsonl") set_default_exporter(exporter_jsonl(trace_file)) with_trace("auto-1", { with_span("work", type = "tool", { 1 + 1 }) }) #> [1] 2 with_trace("auto-2", { with_span("more-work", type = "tool", { 2 + 2 }) }) #> [1] 4 readLines(trace_file) |> length() #> [1] 2 unlink(trace_file)

title: Exporters
text: Pass function exporter(). receives serialized trace list.
code: span_counter <- exporter(function(trace_list) {   cat(sprintf("Trace '%s': %d spans\n",               trace_list$name, length(trace_list$spans))) }) with_trace("custom-export", exporter = span_counter, {   with_span("a", type = "tool", { 1 })   with_span("b", type = "tool", { 2 })   with_span("c", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(100, 50, model = "claude-haiku-4-5")     3   }) }) #> Trace 'custom-export': 3 spans #> [1] 3

title: Exporters
text: Convert trace plain list $to_list() – exporters receive. Discover JSONL fields trace_schema():
code: tr <- Trace$new("serialize-demo") tr$start() s <- Span$new("work", type = "custom") s$start() s$add_metric("quality", 0.95) s$end() tr$add_span(s) tr$end() trace_list <- tr$to_list() names(trace_list) #> [1] "trace_id"      "name"          "status"        "metadata"      #> [5] "resource"      "start_time"    "end_time"      "duration_secs" #> [9] "spans" trace_list$spans[[1]]$metrics #> [[1]] #> [[1]]$name #> [1] "quality" #>  #> [[1]]$value #> [1] 0.95 schema <- trace_schema() names(schema) #> [1] "trace_id"   "name"       "status"     "start_time" "end_time"   #> [6] "duration"   "spans" names(schema$spans$fields) #>  [1] "span_id"       "name"          "type"          "status"        #>  [5] "start_time"    "end_time"      "duration_secs" "parent_id"     #>  [9] "model"         "input_tokens"  "output_tokens"

title: Traces, Spans, and Events
text: Inner spans record outer span’s ID parent_id: Parent-child relationships persist exported JSON via parent_id fields.
code: Trace: "pipeline" |-- Span: "planning" (llm) |   '-- Span: "calculator" (tool)   <- child '-- Span: "summarize" (llm)         <- sibling result <- with_trace("pipeline", {   with_span("planning", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(2000, 500, model = "claude-sonnet-4-5")     with_span("calculator", type = "tool", { 2 + 2 })   })   with_span("summarize", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(1000, 200, model = "claude-haiku-4-5")     "done"   }) })

title: Traces, Spans, and Events
text: Use R6 classes spans created one function ended another, building traces recorded data.
code: tr <- Trace$new("manual-trace", metadata = list(user = "analyst")) tr$start() s1 <- Span$new("llm-call", type = "llm") s1$start() s1$set_model("claude-opus-4-6") s1$set_tokens(input = 5000L, output = 1000L) s1$end() tr$add_span(s1) s2 <- Span$new("tool-use", type = "tool", parent_id = s1$span_id) s2$start() s2$add_metric("rows_processed", 150, unit = "rows") s2$end() tr$add_span(s2) tr$end() tr$status #> [1] "completed" tr$duration() #> [1] 0.006552219 length(tr$spans) #> [1] 2

title: Traces, Spans, and Events
text: Create trace_event(), attach $add_event(), access @:
code: tr <- Trace$new("event-demo") tr$start() s <- Span$new("llm-call", type = "llm") s$start() s$add_event(trace_event("model_selected", data = list(model = "claude-sonnet-4-5"))) s$add_event(trace_event("prompt_sent", data = list(length = 1500L))) s$set_tokens(input = 1500L, output = 300L) s$end() tr$add_span(s) tr$end() length(s$events) #> [1] 2 s$events[[1]]@name #> [1] "model_selected" s$events[[1]]@data #> $model #> [1] "claude-sonnet-4-5"

title: Traces, Spans, and Events
text: Use record_metric() / record_latency() inside with_span(), $add_metric() R6 object:
code: result <- with_trace("metrics-demo", {   with_span("data-processing", type = "tool", {     record_metric("rows_processed", 1500, unit = "rows")     record_latency(0.42)     "processed"   }) }) s <- Span$new("manual-metrics", type = "tool") s$start() s$add_metric("cache_hits", 42, unit = "count") s$end() s$metrics #> NULL

title: Traces, Spans, and Events
text: Errors inside with_span() set status "error" record message. trace still exports, re-raises.
code: trace_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".jsonl") exp <- exporter_jsonl(trace_file) with_trace("error-run", exporter = exp, {   with_span("failing-step", type = "tool", {     stop("something went wrong")   }) }) #> Error in `doTryCatch()`: #> ! something went wrong lines <- readLines(trace_file) trace_data <- jsonlite::fromJSON(lines[[1]]) trace_data$status #> [1] "error" trace_data$spans$status #> [1] "error" trace_data$spans$error #> [1] "something went wrong" unlink(trace_file)

title: Traces, Spans, and Events
text: trace_tool_call() trace_guardrail() create typed spans automatically. require active with_trace().
code: with_trace("tool-integration", {   trace_tool_call("calculator", function(x) x * 2, 21) }) #> [1] 42 with_trace("guard-integration", {   trace_guardrail("length-check", function(x) nchar(x) < 1000, "short text") }) #> [1] TRUE

title: Traces, Spans, and Events
text: trace_execution() wraps securer::SecureSession$execute() span, recording code stdout events:
code: session <- securer::SecureSession$new() with_trace("sandboxed-run", {   trace_execution(session, "cat('hello'); 1 + 1") }) session$close()

title: Traces, Spans, and Events
text: Pass Guard object trace_guardrail() structured result metadata:
code: guard <- secureguard::guard_code_analysis() with_trace("guarded-input", {   trace_guardrail("code-safety", guard, "system('rm -rf /')") })

title: Traces, Spans, and Events
text: Multi-step workflow: nested spans, tokens, events, metrics, JSONL export.
code: trace_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".jsonl") exp <- exporter_multi(exporter_jsonl(trace_file), exporter_console(verbose = TRUE)) result <- with_trace("full-workflow", exporter = exp, {   with_span("planning", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(3000, 800, model = "claude-sonnet-4-5")     current_span()$add_event(trace_event("model_selected",       data = list(model = "claude-sonnet-4-5")))     list(steps = c("fetch", "compute", "summarize"))   })   data <- with_span("fetch-data", type = "tool", {     record_metric("rows_fetched", 250, unit = "rows")     data.frame(x = 1:250, y = rnorm(250))   })   with_span("compute", type = "custom", {     with_span("validate", type = "guardrail", { nrow(data) > 0 })     with_span("transform", type = "tool", { mean(data$y) })   })   with_span("summarize", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(1500, 400, model = "claude-haiku-4-5")     sprintf("Processed %d rows, mean = %.2f", nrow(data), mean(data$y))   }) }) #> --- Trace: full-workflow --- #> Status: completed #> Duration: 0.00s #> Spans: 6 #> -- Spans -- #>   * planning [llm] (ok) - 0.000s #>   * fetch-data [tool] (ok) - 0.000s #>   * compute [custom] (ok) - 0.001s #>   * validate [guardrail] (ok) - 0.000s #>   * transform [tool] (ok) - 0.000s #>   * summarize [llm] (ok) - 0.000s lines <- readLines(trace_file) trace_data <- jsonlite::fromJSON(lines[[1]]) sprintf("Trace '%s': %d spans, status = %s",         trace_data$name, length(trace_data$spans$span_id), trace_data$status) #> [1] "Trace 'full-workflow': 6 spans, status = completed" unlink(trace_file)

title: Traces, Spans, and Events
text: vignette("exporters"): JSONL, console, custom exporters, trace schema. vignette("cloud-native"): OTLP, Prometheus, W3C Trace Context. vignette("orchestr-integration"): automatic tracing orchestr graphs.

title: Tracing orchestr Workflows
text: Auto-instrument every node compiled graph: one span per node, zero handler changes.
code: gb <- graph_builder() gb$add_node("fetch", function(state, config) list(data = mtcars[1:5, ])) gb$add_node("summarize", function(state, config) list(summary = summary(state$data))) gb$add_edge("fetch", "summarize") gb$add_edge("summarize", END) gb$set_entry_point("fetch") graph <- gb$compile()  result <- trace_graph(graph, list(data = NULL, summary = NULL))

title: Tracing orchestr Workflows
text: Wrap single agent invocation; model name token deltas extracted ellmer chat object automatically.
code: chat <- ellmer::chat_openai(model = "gpt-4o") assistant <- agent("assistant", chat) response <- trace_agent(assistant, "Summarize the iris dataset.")

title: Tracing orchestr Workflows
text: Pass exporter persist trace JSONL.
code: exp <- exporter_jsonl(tempfile(fileext = ".jsonl")) result <- trace_graph(graph, list(data = NULL, summary = NULL), exporter = exp)  # Or set a default for all traces set_default_exporter(exporter_jsonl(tempfile(fileext = ".jsonl"))) result <- trace_graph(graph, list(data = NULL, summary = NULL))

title: Tracing orchestr Workflows
text: Aggregate costs across spans. Useful ReAct loops call count unpredictable.
code: chat <- ellmer::chat_openai(model = "gpt-4o") graph <- react_graph(agent("analyst", chat)) result <- trace_graph(graph, list(messages = list("Analyze sales trends."))) trace_total_cost(current_trace())

title: Tracing orchestr Workflows
text: Add with_span() inside handler sub-node detail. Combine trace_graph() freely.
code: analyze_node <- function(state, config) {   with_span("llm-call", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(500, 120, model = "claude-sonnet-4-5")     list(result = "analysis complete")   }) }  gb <- graph_builder() gb$add_node("analyze", analyze_node) gb$add_edge("analyze", END) gb$set_entry_point("analyze")  with_trace("custom-graph-run", {   gb$compile()$invoke(list(result = NULL)) }) #> $result #> [1] "analysis complete"

title: Getting Started with securetrace
text: Wrap workflow with_trace(), break spans, record tokens: with_trace() creates Trace, starts clock, evaluates code, ends trace. with_span() wraps single operation (LLM call, tool use, etc.). record_tokens() logs input/output tokens model current span. Use current_trace() current_span() access active objects anywhere inside block.
code: library(securetrace)  result <- with_trace("my-agent-run", {   with_span("planning", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(1500, 300, model = "claude-sonnet-4-5")     "The answer is 42"   }) })

title: Getting Started with securetrace
text: Built-pricing covers Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Mistral, DeepSeek. Register models: Cloud provider model IDs (Bedrock, Vertex) resolve automatically via resolve_model(): Map internal deployment names add_model_alias():
code: # All known pricing (per 1M tokens) costs <- model_costs() head(names(costs)) #> [1] "claude-opus-4-6"            "claude-sonnet-4-5"          #> [3] "claude-haiku-4-5"           "claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022" #> [5] "claude-3-5-haiku-20241022"  "claude-3-opus-20240229" # Cost for a single call calculate_cost("claude-sonnet-4-5", input_tokens = 5000, output_tokens = 1000) #> [1] 0.03 add_model_cost("my-fine-tuned", input_price = 5, output_price = 20) calculate_cost("my-fine-tuned", input_tokens = 10000, output_tokens = 2000) #> [1] 0.09 calculate_cost(   "anthropic.claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022-v2:0",   input_tokens = 10000, output_tokens = 2000 ) #> [1] 0.06 add_model_alias("my-company-claude", "claude-sonnet-4-5") calculate_cost("my-company-claude", input_tokens = 5000, output_tokens = 1000) #> [1] 0.03

title: Getting Started with securetrace
text: Write traces JSONL downstream analysis: Print console debugging: Set default exporter every with_trace() auto-exports: See vignette("exporters") custom exporters, exporter_multi(), JSONL schema reference.
code: exp <- exporter_jsonl(tempfile("traces", fileext = ".jsonl"))  with_trace("exported-run", exporter = exp, {   with_span("work", type = "tool", { 42 }) }) #> [1] 42 debug_exp <- exporter_console(verbose = TRUE)  with_trace("debug-run", exporter = debug_exp, {   with_span("step", type = "custom", { 1 + 1 }) }) #> --- Trace: debug-run --- #> Status: completed #> Duration: 0.00s #> Spans: 1 #> -- Spans -- #>   * step [custom] (ok) - 0.000s #> [1] 2 set_default_exporter(exp)

title: Getting Started with securetrace
text: Call $summary() completed trace see duration, span count, tokens, cost glance:
code: tr <- Trace$new("summarized-run") tr$start() s <- Span$new("llm", type = "llm") s$start() s$set_tokens(input = 5000, output = 1000) s$set_model("claude-opus-4-6") s$end() tr$add_span(s) tr$end()  tr$summary() #> Trace: summarized-run (completed) ID: 163a3181d2b2f09bcf64302605ab6a97 #> Duration: 0.00s Spans: 1 Tokens: 5000 input, 1000 output Cost: $0.150000

title: Observability and Tracing for Large Language Model Agent Workflows
text: Structured traces – OpenTelemetry-inspired traces spans R Token accounting – Track input/output tokens per span Cost calculation – Built-pricing Claude GPT models JSONL export – Write traces structured log files Context management – Automatic trace/span stacking with_trace() / with_span() Integration helpers – Wrappers LLM calls, tool executions, guardrail checks Prometheus metrics – Counters, histograms, HTTP /metrics endpoint

title: Observability and Tracing for Large Language Model Agent Workflows
text: securetrace ships built-pricing Claude GPT models. Record tokens per span calculate costs automatically:
code: library(securetrace)  # Record tokens and compute cost within a trace result <- with_trace("cost-demo", {   with_span("llm-call", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(1500, 300, model = "claude-sonnet-4-5")     "response text"   }) })  # Calculate cost for a single call (price per 1M tokens) calculate_cost("gpt-4o", input_tokens = 1000, output_tokens = 500)  # Register custom model pricing add_model_cost("my-local-model", input_price = 0, output_price = 0)  # Sum costs across all spans in a trace tr <- current_trace() trace_total_cost(tr)

title: Observability and Tracing for Large Language Model Agent Workflows
text: securetrace provides wrappers auto-instrument calls ellmer, securer, secureguard:
code: # Trace an ellmer LLM call (requires ellmer) chat <- ellmer::chat_openai(model = "gpt-4o") with_trace("agent-run", {   response <- trace_llm_call(chat, "What is 2 + 2?") })  # Trace a tool function call with_trace("tool-run", {   result <- trace_tool_call("add", function(a, b) a + b, 3, 4) })  # Trace a guardrail check with_trace("guard-run", {   result <- trace_guardrail("length_check", function(x) nchar(x) < 1000, "input") })  # Trace a securer sandbox execution (requires securer) session <- securer::SecureSession$new() with_trace("sandbox-run", {   result <- trace_execution(session, "1 + 1") }) session$close()

title: Observability and Tracing for Large Language Model Agent Workflows
text: Write traces JSONL files, print console, combine multiple exporters:
code: # JSONL exporter -- one JSON object per line exp <- jsonl_exporter("traces.jsonl") with_trace("my-run", exporter = exp, {   with_span("step-1", type = "tool", { 42 }) })  # Console exporter -- human-readable summary with_trace("my-run", exporter = console_exporter(verbose = TRUE), {   with_span("step-1", type = "tool", { 42 }) })  # Multi-exporter -- fan out to several destinations combined <- multi_exporter(   console_exporter(verbose = FALSE),   jsonl_exporter("traces.jsonl") ) with_trace("my-run", exporter = combined, {   with_span("step-1", type = "tool", { 42 }) })  # Set a default exporter for all with_trace() calls set_default_exporter(jsonl_exporter("all-traces.jsonl"))

title: Observability and Tracing for Large Language Model Agent Workflows
text: Expose trace metrics Prometheus text exposition format scraping Prometheus, Grafana, compatible monitoring system:
code: # Collect metrics from a trace into a registry reg <- prometheus_registry() tr <- Trace$new("demo") tr$start() s <- Span$new("llm-step", type = "llm") s$start() s$set_model("gpt-4o") s$set_tokens(input = 100L, output = 50L) s$end() tr$add_span(s) tr$end()  prometheus_metrics(tr, reg) cat(format_prometheus(reg))  # Or use the prometheus_exporter() with with_trace() exp <- prometheus_exporter() with_trace("agent-run", exporter = exp, {   with_span("call", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(500, 200, model = "gpt-4o")   }) })  # Serve a /metrics HTTP endpoint (requires httpuv) srv <- serve_prometheus(reg, port = 9090) # Scrape http://localhost:9090/metrics httpuv::stopServer(srv)

text: New vignette ecosystem-integration.Rmd — canonical reference sibling packages (securer, securetools, secureguard, securecontext, orchestr, securebench) emit spans securetrace context. Includes span taxonomy table template writing sibling-style instrumentation.

title: securetrace 0.2.0
text: Sampling: Control traces recorded pluggable samplers. sampler_always_on(), sampler_always_off(), sampler_probability(), sampler_rate_limiting() cover common strategies. Set default set_default_sampler(). Span attributes: Span$set_attribute(key, value) provides typed key-value attribute API, separate freeform metadata field. Attributes included to_list() exporters. Resource attributes: resource() set_resource() attach service-level metadata (service name, version, deployment environment) traces. Resources appear exported trace data.

title: securetrace 0.2.0
text: ecosystem packages (secureguard, securetools, securer, securecontext, securebench) now emit spans automatically securetrace installed trace active. manual instrumentation needed. Pre-built Grafana dashboard docker-compose setup turnkey observability Prometheus metrics.

title: securetrace 0.1.0
text: Initial CRAN release. Core tracing: Trace Span R6 classes structured observability. Trace events via S7-based securetrace_event class. Token cost accounting built-pricing OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, Mistral, DeepSeek models. Cloud provider model alias resolution (AWS Bedrock, Google Vertex AI). Exporters: JSONL, console, multi-exporter, OTLP JSON OpenTelemetry-compatible collectors (Jaeger, Grafana Tempo). OTLP batching configurable buffer size retry exponential backoff transient HTTP errors. Prometheus metrics: registry, text exposition format, HTTP /metrics endpoint via httpuv. W3C Trace Context propagation: traceparent(), parse_traceparent(), inject_headers(), extract_trace_context(). Integration helpers ellmer (trace_llm_call()), securer (trace_execution()), secureguard (trace_guardrail()), orchestr (trace_graph(), trace_agent()). Context management: with_trace(), with_span(), current_trace(), current_span(), set_default_exporter().
